uplink.description: Upstream example: fixed M-Bus telegram (CI=0x73), Water meter, actual volume 1 l
uplink._note: From acr-cv-101l-m-x-codec.yaml uplinkDecoder.examples. Storage-0 actual counter is 1 l; expected.data derived by running reference/upstream-codec.js (records[0].value=1, unit l) and mapping to metering.water.total (l x 1).

uplink.description: Fixed telegram (CI=0x73), Water meter, actual volume 987654 l
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds fixed frame (BCD counter 987654 l, valid M-Bus checksum). Upstream oracle decodes records[0].value=987654 unit l -> metering.water.total 987654.

uplink.description: Fixed telegram (CI=0x73), Water meter, zero volume (lower bound)
uplink._note: Synthetic zero-volume fixed frame. Upstream oracle decodes records[0].value=0 unit l -> metering.water.total 0 (>= 0 bound).

uplink.description: Variable telegram (CI=0x72), Water meter, Volume record 12.345 m3
uplink._note: Synthetic variable-data frame: DIF 0x0C (8-digit BCD) + VIF 0x13 (Volume, m3 x 1e-3) over raw BCD 12345 -> 12.345 m3. Upstream oracle: records[0].type=Volume, unit m3, value 12.345 -> metering.water.total 12345 (m3 x 1000).

uplink.description: Fixed telegram with Power Low application status (status bit 0x04)
uplink._note: Synthetic fixed frame, actual volume 5000 l, M-Bus status byte 0x04 (Power Low). Upstream oracle records the M-Bus application status 'Power Low'; we surface it as a normalized warning.
Upstream front-end only decodes the (1,1) single-frame case; any other index/count pair is a multi-frame fragment that cannot be decoded on-device.

uplink.description: Corrupt M-Bus telegram (checksum mismatch) is rejected
uplink._note: Same payload as the Power-Low frame but with the M-Bus checksum byte zeroed, so the ported tmbus frame validator throws 'Check sum failed'.
